original_reasoning: Arabic is to twist tightly; Greek is to take prisoner. Binding and captivity form a clear action-to-result chain.
Sniffing/dry vs 'arid stalk, dry twig' - direct dryness match, strong link
Arabic gives zeal, ardor, and bravery; Greek is spear points. Weapon and martial valor form a clear war-domain chain.
Arabic gloss is a wild tree/plant and Greek is wild leek; the shared botanical-wild domain is a clear semantic chain.
دفع and a kind of intercourse both preserve exchange or mutual taking, close to receive from another.
Arabic includes pot-foam whitening on boiling, which gives a real conceptual bridge to Greek paint in white.
سقت 'without blessing/lacking' stands against the imperative 'have, hold'; this is a clear negation pair.

Arabic الثنط: splitting/cracking (in a hadith: 'when the earth was spread out, it split'). Greek Θάνατος: Death. Death as the great splitting/tearing — the Arabic root means the very act that cosmologically creates boundaries, death being the ultimate splitting. Phonological match th-n-t vs. th-n-t.
